lesson: Warm exhaust with the fan clearly running but almost no air getting through means the condenser is blinded by dust. Clean it — the refrigerant was never the problem, so regassing is the expensive miss.
symptoms: Both bowls churning but the slush is just cold syrup.
Machine blowing warm air out the front.
Fan sounds like it's working overtime.

tests.error-log: Refrigeration protection trips logged repeatedly — pressures and temps drifting out of range under load.
tests.temp-probe: The condenser side is running very hot, and almost no air is moving through the back of the machine.

symptoms: Kick plate hot enough to notice through shoes.
Machine cuts out on hot afternoons, limps back after an hour.
Slush never sets properly any more.

flavour: Cured a refrigeration failure with a stiff brush.
authenticityNote: Corner-shop slushie machines get shoved against a wall and suck in dust all day; a blocked condenser is the number one cause of warm slush. Regassing is the classic expensive misdiagnosis — the refrigerant was fine, it just couldn't reject heat.
